summary: cookie-parser-js typosquats expressjs/cookie-parser and fetches+evals attacker-controlled JavaScript
details: Package name and package.json metadata (author TJ Holowaychuk, repository expressjs/cookie-parser, description) impersonate the widely-used `cookie-parser` Express middleware. When the exported `cookieParser` factory is invoked — the standard middleware setup pattern executed as the consuming application initializes — the module performs `https.get` against a base64-obfuscated URL that decodes to https://cookie-api-two.vercel.app/, then passes the fetched JSON's `data.content` field to `eval`. Any application that requires this package and mounts it as middleware executes arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript in the server process, yielding remote code execution on the installer's host. The base64 wrapping of the URL and the impersonation of an unrelated legitimate publisher confirm malicious intent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
